title: Harden test suite — coverage gaps, quality issues, and missing scenarios
description:
## Problem

The test suite has significant coverage gaps, unused fixtures, quality issues, and missing parametrize opportunities. This issue tracks all test improvements.

## A. Coverage Gaps — Untested Code Paths

### `src/dagshund/__init__.py` — `parse_plan`
- Empty string input (`""`) — exercises `json.JSONDecodeError` path
- Numeric JSON literal (`"42"`) — should raise "must be an object"
- Boolean/null JSON literal (`"true"`, `"null"`) — should raise "must be an object"

### `src/dagshund/cli.py` — `read_plan`
- Directory instead of file — `IsADirectoryError` (subclass of `OSError`)
- Non-UTF8 file — `UnicodeDecodeError` (once Bug #4 from input hardening issue is fixed)

### `src/dagshund/cli.py` — `main`
- Invalid JSON piped via stdin → should produce DagshundError on stderr + exit code 1
- `--output` to a read-only/unwritable directory
- Stdin + `--output` flag combined (stdin → HTML export path is completely untested)

### `src/dagshund/text.py` — `_render_resource`
- All `UPDATE_ACTIONS` besides `"update"`: test `recreate`, `resize`, `update_id` — these should show field changes but are never tested
- Missing `action` key entirely — tests the `entry.get("action", "")` fallback
- Non-dict `changes` value (once Bug #1 from input hardening issue is fixed)
- Field change with `action` but no `old`/`new` — exercises has_old/has_new branches
- Field change with missing `action` key

### `src/dagshund/text.py` — other functions
- `_count_by_action({})` — empty input
- `_print_resource_groups` with multiple resource types (only tested with single type "jobs")
- `_print_resource_groups` with empty dict
- `_print_summary` with empty plan
- `_print_summary` with all-same-action
- `_display_action` for an UPDATE_ACTIONS member (e.g., `"update"` → `"update"` unchanged)
- `_format_value` with exactly 80-char string (boundary) and 81-char string (just over)
- `_parse_resource_key` with empty string `""`

### `src/dagshund/text.py` — `render_text`
- Test with `invalid-plan.json` fixture (unknown action type + malformed changes)
- Test with `mixed-plan.json` fixture (create/update/delete/skip mix)
- Test with `sample-plan.json` fixture (single create-only plan)

### `src/dagshund/browser.py`
- `_escape_for_script_tag("")` — empty string edge case
- `_inject_plan` where plan data contains the PLACEHOLDER string as a value
- `render_browser` overwriting an existing file

### Cross-module / Integration
- Subprocess test with `--output` flag (HTML output mode end-to-end)
- Subprocess test piping stdin instead of file argument
- `render_text` + color env var interaction (control FORCE_COLOR/NO_COLOR in tests)

## B. Three Unused Fixtures — Wire Them Up
These fixtures exist but are never used in any test:
- `fixtures/mixed-plan.json` — has create, update, delete, skip actions
- `fixtures/sample-plan.json` — minimal create-only plan
- `fixtures/invalid-plan.json` — has `"action": "invalid_action_type"` and `"changes": "should_be_object"`

## C. Test Quality Fixes

### Replace mock with monkeypatch
- `tests/test_cli.py`, around line 132: `test_main_browser_flag_opens_browser` uses `unittest.mock.patch("webbrowser.open")` instead of monkeypatch
- Fix: `import webbrowser` at top of test, then `monkeypatch.setattr(webbrowser, "open", lambda url: None)`

### Replace manual try/finally with monkeypatch
- `tests/test_browser.py`, around line 24: `test_find_template_raises_when_missing` manually saves/restores `browser_mod.__file__` with try/finally
- Fix: Use `monkeypatch.setattr(browser_mod, "__file__", str(tmp_path / "browser.py"))`

### Parametrize opportunities (reduce duplication)
1. `tests/test_text.py` — three `_display_action` tests (skip→unchanged, empty→unchanged, create→create) → single parametrized test
2. `tests/test_text.py` — four `_all_unchanged` tests → single parametrized test
3. `tests/test_browser.py` — four `_escape_for_script_tag` tests → single parametrized test
4. `tests/test_init.py` — `test_parse_plan_array_raises` and `test_parse_plan_string_raises` → single parametrized test

### Fix brittle assertion
- `tests/test_browser.py`, around line 91: `test_inject_plan_uses_compact_json` checks `'", ' not in result` on the entire HTML string — could false-positive if template HTML contains `", "`
- Fix: Extract just the injected JSON portion and check that, not the entire result

### Naming convention
Several tests don't follow `test_<function>_<scenario>_<expected>`:
- `test_cli_version_flag` → `test_main_version_flag_prints_version`
- `test_cli_text_mode_with_file` → `test_main_text_mode_with_file_prints_output`
- Parametrized tests like `test_action_style`, `test_parse_resource_key`, `test_format_value` should have scenario/expected in the name

## Acceptance Criteria
- All listed coverage gaps have tests
- All three unused fixtures are used in at least one test
- All quality issues fixed (monkeypatch, parametrize, brittle assertions, naming)
- `just check` passes
- No `from unittest.mock import` in any test file

title: refactor: make GraphNode a discriminated union by nodeKind
description: R3-MED-8. GraphNode in types/graph-types.ts is a single type for 4 node kinds (job, task, resource, resource-group). Fields like taskKey, taskChangeSummary, external are undefined for most kinds. Components do runtime checks like data.nodeKind === 'job' && data.taskChangeSummary \!== undefined. CLAUDE.md says 'discriminated unions over type assertions.' Fix: split into JobNodeData, TaskNodeData, ResourceNodeData, ResourceGroupNodeData with a GraphNode = union. Each variant only has fields relevant to that kind. This makes impossible states unrepresentable. Flagged by: Architect (LOW), Code Quality (MEDIUM).

title: Handle xdg-open/browser-open failure gracefully
description: Bun.spawn([detectOpenCommand(), ...]) in src/index.ts:70 and src/cli.ts:221 has no error handling. On Ubuntu without a default browser, xdg-open dumps a wall of 'not found' errors. Should catch the failure and print a friendly message like 'Could not open browser. Visit http://... manually'.

title: perf: improve hover rendering performance in graph views
description: Multiple compounding performance issues on hover: (1) No React.memo on any of the 4 node components — every hover re-renders all nodes via context. (2) useNodeConnections receives new object literals every render in all 4 node components — hoist to module-level constants. (3) styledEdges spreads every edge into a new object on hover and unnecessarily spreads in the null/null case — return baseEdges directly when no styling needed. (4) Event handlers recreated every render — wrap in useCallback. Files: job-node.tsx, task-node.tsx, resource-node.tsx, resource-group-node.tsx, App.tsx, resources-view.tsx.

title: fix: formatValue conflates undefined and null
description: formatValue in utils/format-value.ts returns 'null' for both undefined and null. In diff context, field absent (undefined) vs field explicitly null are different. Fix: return 'undefined' or '<absent>' for undefined, 'null' for null.

title: style: default value format to YAML instead of JSON
Improve detail panel formatting for create/delete diffs
description: When viewing a created (added) resource in the detail panel, the per-field cards show each field's value via CreateOnlyView which just calls formatValue() on the raw value. For object/array values this dumps everything as a single + blob with no structure. Example: check_duplicates shows 'check_duplicatesadded' in the header (name and badge run together), and nested fields like depends_on, notebook_task, task_key are all inlined as flat text rather than displayed as labeled key-value entries. Need to: (1) fix spacing between the field label and the diff state badge in ObjectStateCard/StateFieldRow headers, (2) render object values in CreateOnlyView/DeleteOnlyView with per-key breakdown instead of a single blob.

title: feat: static HTML export mode for CI/CD and local viewing
description:
Add a CLI mode that renders a plan into a self-contained HTML file with all JS/CSS inlined. The plan JSON is embedded as a <script> tag so no server is needed — React boots up and provides the full interactive experience (pan, zoom, click nodes, hover, filter, JSON/YAML toggle).

## Distribution
Ship as an npm/bun package with a bin field in package.json. Users run:
- bunx dagshund plan.json (opens in browser)
- bunx dagshund plan.json -o report.html (saves file for CI artifacts)
- databricks bundle plan -o json | bunx dagshund -o report.html (pipeline-friendly)

No Docker, no server, no runtime infrastructure. CI pipelines already have Node/Bun.

## Implementation
1. New src/cli.ts entry point — arg parsing, reads plan JSON from file arg or stdin
2. Add bin field to package.json pointing to cli.ts
3. CLI runs Bun bundler to produce JS/CSS, inlines everything into a single HTML file
4. Plan JSON injected as window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ script tag
5. Frontend checks window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ first, falls back to fetch(/api/plan) for dev server mode
6. npm publish makes it available via bunx/npx

## Future
Standalone binary via bun build --compile can be added later for environments without Node/Bun.

title: Stdin pipe input: accept plan JSON and open browser
description:
Accept databricks bundle plan JSON via stdin pipe and serve the webview.

USAGE PATTERN:
  databricks bundle plan -o json | dagshund
  # or from a file:
  cat plan.json | dagshund

WHAT EXISTS:
- src/index.ts — Bun server entry point (currently just serves static HTML)
- parsePlanFromString in parser/ — validates JSON string into Plan

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. Detect stdin pipe in src/index.ts — if process.stdin is not a TTY, read all stdin to string
2. Parse the stdin JSON using parsePlanFromString
3. Inject the parsed plan into the served HTML as a script tag: window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ = <json>
4. Auto-open the browser after server starts (use Bun.spawn or child_process to open localhost URL)
5. Frontend (src/frontend.tsx or App.tsx) reads window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ on mount and feeds it to the graph

FALLBACK: If no stdin, serve the app anyway with empty state (for dev mode / future file upload)

DATA CONTEXT:
- Plan JSON can be large (hundreds of resources with nested tasks and changes records)
- The window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ global holds the raw parsed Plan object
- The rendering layer (dagshund-qu9) reads this and passes it through the graph model

CONSTRAINTS:
- Handle parse errors gracefully — print to stderr and exit with code 1
- Use Result type from types/result.ts
- No global mutable state — read stdin once, inject into HTML response
- Server should pick an available port if default is taken (nice to have, not blocking)
close_reason: Stdin pipe input implemented: server reads stdin, parses with Zod, serves at /api/plan. Frontend fetches dynamically with loading/empty/error/ready states. Auto-opens browser on pipe.

title: Harden input validation — crashes on malformed plan data
description:
## Problem

Four crash bugs in the Python package where malformed/unexpected input causes unhandled exceptions (raw tracebacks) instead of friendly DagshundError messages.

## Bug 1: `_render_resource` crashes on non-dict `changes`
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around line 113
- The code does `for field_name, change in sorted(changes.items())` assuming `changes` is a dict
- If `changes` is a string, list, int, or any non-dict truthy value AND the action is in `UPDATE_ACTIONS`, it crashes with `AttributeError: 'str' object has no attribute 'items'`
- The guard `if changes and action in UPDATE_ACTIONS` only checks truthiness, not type
- The `fixtures/invalid-plan.json` fixture has `"changes": "should_be_object"` — it only dodges the crash because its action is `"invalid_action_type"` (not in UPDATE_ACTIONS)
- **Fix**: Add an `isinstance(changes, dict)` check before iterating, or silently skip non-dict changes

## Bug 2: Individual change entries assumed to be dicts
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around line 114
- Inside the changes loop, `change.get("action", "")` assumes each change VALUE is a dict
- If a change value is a string, None, or integer → `AttributeError`
- **Fix**: Add `isinstance(change, dict)` guard or skip non-dict change values

## Bug 3: `render_text` crashes when `plan` value isn't a dict
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around lines 201-208
- If JSON has `"plan": "not_a_dict"` or `"plan": [...]`, the truthiness check passes
- Then `_all_unchanged(plan)` calls `plan.values()` → `AttributeError`
- Same for plan entries that are None or strings — downstream functions call `.get()` on them
- **Fix**: Validate `isinstance(plan, dict)` after extracting it, raise DagshundError if not

## Bug 4: `read_plan` doesn't catch `UnicodeDecodeError`
- **File**: `src/dagshund/cli.py`, around line 54
- `open(..., encoding="utf-8")` raises `UnicodeDecodeError` on binary/corrupt files
- `UnicodeDecodeError` inherits from `ValueError`, NOT `OSError`
- Neither `except FileNotFoundError` nor `except OSError` catches it
- Users get a raw Python traceback instead of a friendly DagshundError
- **Fix**: Add `except UnicodeDecodeError` or `except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError)` that wraps it in DagshundError

## Acceptance Criteria
- All four bugs produce a friendly DagshundError message instead of a raw traceback
- `just check` passes after fixes
- Do NOT add tests in this issue — tests are tracked separately in the test hardening issue
close_reason: All four crash bugs fixed with guards + 5 new tests, 99 passing, 100% coverage

title: Audit and improve handling of skip/unchanged resources
description:
The text renderer shows 'skip' to the user (e.g. '8 skip') which is Databricks internal terminology. Users see 'unchanged' — we should display that instead.

## Scope
1. **Text mode**: 'skip' action renders as '(skip)' and '8 skip' in summary. Should say 'unchanged'.
2. **Browser mode**: verify how skip resources render in the DAG visualization — are they styled as 'unchanged'?
3. **All-skip plan**: when every resource is skip (no changes), the CLI currently renders the full plan with all resources listed. Should it show a 'no changes' summary instead? Or at least suppress the resource list?

## Fixture
`fixtures/no-changes-plan.json` — real Databricks output where nothing changed, all 8 resources have action 'skip'.

## Current behavior
```
dagshund plan (v2, cli 0.288.0)

  alerts (1)
    alerts/stale_pipeline_alert  (skip)
  ...
   8 skip
```

## Related
- dagshund-qwui: detailed exit codes (needs to distinguish all-skip from real changes)
- dagshund-cfx: empty plan error (different from all-skip — empty plan = no plan key)
close_reason: Python text mode: skip→unchanged mapping, all-unchanged short-circuit, clean style map. Browser side tracked in dagshund-wtjp.

Determine intended behavior and make consistent.

title: Fix action:null inconsistency across three functions
description:
## Problem

Three functions in `src/dagshund/text.py` disagree on how to interpret `"action": null` (JSON null / Python None) in a resource entry, producing inconsistent output.

## The Three Functions and Their Behavior

### 1. `_render_resource` (around line 101)
- Uses `entry.get("action", "")` → returns `None` (not the default, because key exists with value None)
- `_display_action(None)` → returns `None` unchanged (not in `_DISPLAY_ACTIONS`, falls through)
- Resource displayed as `? jobs/test  (None)` with unknown-action style

### 2. `_count_by_action` (around line 138)
- Uses `entry.get("action") or "unchanged"` → `None or "unchanged"` → `"unchanged"`
- Summary counts it as "unchanged"

### 3. `_all_unchanged` (around line 194)
- Uses `entry.get("action", "")` → returns `None`
- Checks `None in ("skip", "")` → `False`
- So it does NOT consider the resource unchanged, meaning the full resource listing is shown

## Result
- Summary line says "unchanged"
- Resource listing shows "?/None" with unknown-action styling
- `_all_unchanged` says "not all unchanged" so the listing IS shown (contradicting the summary)

## Reproduction
```python
from dagshund.text import render_text
import json
render_text(json.dumps({"plan": {"resources.jobs.test": {"action": None}}}))
```

## Design Decision Needed
What should `action: null` mean? Options:
1. Treat it the same as `action: ""` and `action: "skip"` → "unchanged"
2. Treat it as an unknown/error action

Either way, all three functions must agree. Recommendation: treat null the same as missing/empty → "unchanged", since Databricks CLI may omit the field or set it to null for no-op resources.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All three functions (`_render_resource`, `_count_by_action`, `_all_unchanged`) handle `action: null` consistently
- The chosen behavior is explicit, not accidental (e.g., normalize early rather than relying on falsy checks)
- `just check` passes
close_reason: Aligned _count_by_action to use same extraction pattern as _render_resource and _all_unchanged — null actions now consistently treated as unknown

title: fix: clean up modified task detail panel — remove redundant diff state display
description:
The detail panel for modified tasks is visually noisy with redundant information:

## Current behavior
- Top bar shows task name + "modified" action badge
- Below that, a "Modified" text banner repeats the same info
- Then the actual changed fields
- Then an "Unchanged" separator at the bottom

## Desired behavior
- Top bar: task name + "modified" action badge (keep as-is)
- Body: "Modified" and "Unchanged" section dividers organizing the fields
- No extra banner/block that redundantly restates the diff state

Added and removed nodes are clean — single wrapped block with a tag. Modified tasks should follow the same clean pattern but with section dividers to separate changed vs unchanged fields.

title: Smarter diff display for modified resources in detail panel
description:
When clicking a modified node, the detail panel currently shows full before/after arrays for fields like depends_on. For example:

- [{ task_key: check_nulls }]
+ [{ task_key: check_nulls }, { task_key: check_duplicates }]

This should be reduced to just showing the meaningful delta:
+ check_duplicates

However, this is not always straightforward — need to think about validity/edge cases:
- What if items were reordered but not added/removed?
- What if items were both added AND removed in the same array?
- What about arrays of primitives vs arrays of objects?
- How do we match identity across old/new arrays (by which key)?
- Nested object diffs within array items

Needs a design pass before implementation to handle these cases correctly.

title: epic: UI overhaul — Databricks-inspired design with dark/light mode
description:
Graphical overhaul of the entire application to bring a cohesive, polished visual style inspired by the Databricks UI aesthetic.

## Goals
- Adopt a Databricks-inspired design language (clean lines, subtle shadows, professional color palette)
- Implement both dark mode and light mode with a user toggle
- Consistent styling across all views (DAG, resources, detail panel, upload screen)

## Scope
- Theming infrastructure (CSS variables or Tailwind theme, mode toggle, persistence)
- Restyle all components: nodes, edges, panels, buttons, inputs, headers
- Diff state colors adapted for both modes (green/red/amber must be readable in both)
- React Flow canvas styling (background, controls, minimap)
- Typography, spacing, and layout polish

title: chore: add missing barrel exports to types/index.ts
description: R3-LOW-29. Several exported types are missing from the types/index.ts barrel: TaskChangeSummaryEntry (from graph-types.ts), BaselineLabel (from structural-diff.ts), ArrayElementStatus (from structural-diff.ts). Consumers must import directly from module files instead of the barrel. Fix: add these to the re-exports in types/index.ts. Flagged by: Code Quality (MEDIUM/LOW).

title: Test dagshund with PyDABS-generated bundle plans
description: Validate that dagshund correctly parses and visualizes plan JSON generated from PyDABS dynamic bundle definitions. PyDABS generates Databricks asset bundle YAML dynamically, which then feeds into databricks bundle plan. Ensure the plan output from these dynamically-generated bundles renders correctly — edge cases may include unusual resource structures or dependency patterns that static YAML wouldn't produce.
close_reason: deferred — no PyDABS plans available for testing right now

title: Use hatchling dynamic versioning instead of dual version
description: Version is hardcoded in both __init__.py and pyproject.toml. Use hatchling's [tool.hatch.version] to read from __init__.py dynamically, eliminating the second source of truth.

title: Scrub fake email from fixtures and git history
description: A fake email appears in all test fixtures, the generator script, and test-bundle. Replace with user@example.com in current files. Optionally git filter-repo to clean history (requires force push). Affects: js/tests/fixtures/*.json, js/tests/fixtures/generate-large-fixture.ts, js/test-bundle/real_fixture.json, js/test-bundle/databricks.yml.
close_reason: Email was temporary and already gone, no action needed

title: style: dashed borders for removed nodes/edges, remove reduced opacity
fix: use .toSorted() instead of .sort() in diffObjects
description: R3-MED-17. structural-diff.ts:214 uses entries.sort(...) which mutates the array in place. The rest of the codebase correctly uses .toSorted() (e.g. build-task-change-summary.ts:68). One-line fix: change entries.sort((a, b) => ...) to entries.toSorted((a, b) => ...) and return the result. Also add a comment to diffArrays acknowledging local mutation is deliberate for the matching algorithm. Flagged by: Code Quality (MEDIUM).

title: fix: escape CSS content for style tag in static HTML export
description: cli.ts inlines CSS into <style> tag without escaping </style sequences. If CSS ever contained </style, it would break out. Fix: add .replace(/<\/style/gi, '<\/style') to CSS before inlining, same pattern as the script tag escaping.

title: refactor: extract useNodeDimming hook from 4 node components
description: Identical dimming logic (useHoverState, useNodeConnections, isDimmedByHover, isDimmedByFilter, isFilterHighlighted, opacityClass) duplicated in task-node.tsx, job-node.tsx, resource-node.tsx, resource-group-node.tsx. Also TARGET_HANDLE/SOURCE_HANDLE constants duplicated. Extract a useNodeDimming(id, diffState) hook returning { isDimmed, opacityClass, styles, incomingConnections, outgoingConnections }.

title: fix: non-job resources appear as job nodes in DAG tab
description: buildPlanGraph processes ALL plan entries through buildEntryGraph, not just resources.jobs.*. Schemas, volumes, alerts etc get rendered as empty job containers in the jobs DAG view. Fix: filter entries to key.startsWith('resources.jobs.') in buildPlanGraph.

title: task: restyle detail panel — side panel, change viewer, value display
description:
Restyle the slide-in detail panel and all its sub-components. Part of epic dagshund-4y6.

## Deliverables
- Panel background, border, and shadow for both modes
- Change viewer (diff display) with themed add/remove colors
- JSON/YAML value display with syntax-appropriate coloring
- Section headers, dividers, and spacing updated
- Close button and panel header styled
- Scrollbar styling if visible

title: fix: simplify job detail panel in resources tab
description: When clicking a job node on the Resources tab, the detail panel is overwhelming. It shows the full Modified section with all task-level changes (depends_on diffs, new tasks, field changes) AND the full Unchanged section with every field (deployment, environments, all tasks with their full config). This is too much for the Resources tab — the Jobs tab already provides the detailed task-level DAG view. The Resources tab should show a condensed summary for jobs: just the top-level resource state (name, format, max_concurrent_runs, etc.) and a high-level change summary (e.g. '3 tasks added, 2 tasks modified') with a link/button to navigate to the Jobs tab for full details.

title: fix: add assertion that __DAGSHUND_PLAN_JSON__ placeholder was actually replaced
description: R3-MED-9. The Python/JS boundary uses a stringly-typed placeholder __DAGSHUND_PLAN_JSON__ in template HTML. Python does .replace() to inject real JSON. If the placeholder string changes in build-template.ts but not in browser.py (or vice versa), the replacement silently fails — the app loads but shows a parse error. Fix: in browser.py _inject_plan, add an assertion that the placeholder is no longer present in the output after replacement. Also consider adding a test that verifies the round-trip works. The placeholder is defined in browser.py:7 and build-template.ts:8. Flagged by: Architect (MEDIUM).

title: refactor: split detail-panel.tsx into directory
description: detail-panel.tsx is 482 lines with 16 co-located components (ActionBadge, DiffStateBadge, ChangeEntry, ResourceStateView, SectionDivider, ObjectStateCard, TaskChangesSummary, etc). CLAUDE.md says each component in its own file. Extract into components/detail-panel/ directory with index.tsx as the main export.

title: fix: make deepEqual key-order-independent in structural-diff
description: structural-diff.ts:12 uses JSON.stringify(a) === JSON.stringify(b) for deep equality. JSON.stringify is key-order-dependent, so {a:1,b:2} != {b:2,a:1}. This can produce false negatives (phantom diffs) when plan JSON has different key ordering. Also O(n^2) when used in diffArrays findIndex loop. Fix: use a proper key-order-independent deep-equal, and consider building a Map for the fallback matching instead of O(n*m) findIndex.

title: Implement Zod parser + types for plan JSON
Simplify ResourceChangesByType to compose with ResourceChanges
description: ResourceChangesByType is currently dict[ResourceType, list[tuple[ResourceKey, ResourceChange]]] but could be dict[ResourceType, ResourceChanges] — sub-dicts instead of lists of tuples. The tuple form exists because groupby yields pairs, but building sub-dicts would make the types compose naturally and simplify _print_resource_groups to iterate .items() instead of a tuple list.

title: Add test infrastructure and parser tests
chore: remove dead code and fix micro-inconsistencies
description: Cleanup items: (1) formatValue (format-value.ts) has redundant typeof value==='string' branch — both paths return identical yamlStringify(value).trimEnd(). (2) isFilterHighlighted uses optional chaining in resource-node.tsx but explicit \!== null && in job-node/task-node — make consistent. (3) Unused barrel exports: TaskChangeSummaryEntry, DependsOnEntry, BaselineLabel, ArrayElementStatus — remove from types/index.ts. (4) useResourceGraph not exported from hooks barrel. (5) Barrel files exist but are almost universally bypassed by direct imports — decide on a convention.

title: fix: remove truncate class from detail panel header h2
description: R3-MED-21. The detail panel header shows the node label with CSS truncate class, cutting off long resource names. Nodes in the graph need truncation (fixed NODE_WIDTH=220), but the detail panel header has plenty of room — it should show the full name. Fix: remove the 'truncate' class from the h2 in components/detail-panel/detail-panel.tsx around line 51. Allow the text to wrap instead. Flagged by: UX (MEDIUM).

title: fix: rewrite groupNodesByJob to be immutable (no .push mutation)
description: R3-HIGH-5. groupNodesByJob in layout-graph.ts:32-56 mutates objects in place: .push(node) on tasks array and reassignment of job field inside a Map. Directly violates the project's immutability-by-default principle. Fix: use functional approach with Map.groupBy: const jobs = nodes.filter(n => n.nodeKind === 'job'); const tasksByResource = Map.groupBy(tasks, t => t.resourceKey); return jobs.map(job => ({ job, tasks: tasksByResource.get(job.resourceKey) ?? [] })). Also add a comment to topologicalSortTasks acknowledging that Kahn's algorithm uses local mutable state for algorithmic efficiency (deliberate exception). Flagged by: Code Quality (HIGH).

title: Fix bun --hot HMR reload loop with elkjs
description: bun run dev triggers continuous hot reloads when elkjs is in the dependency graph. The HMR re-bundles layout-graph.ts (~2600ms each) in an infinite loop. Production build works fine. Likely a bun HMR bug with large bundled dependencies. Workaround: use bun run build + static server for now.
close_reason: Verified resolved — no HMR reload loop observed with elkjs in dependency graph. User confirmed no flickering in browser.

title: Fix viewport centering offset on first node click
description: In src/components/flow-canvas.tsx:60-73, the click handler uses node.measured?.width which may not be available on first click after layout. Falls back to hardcoded 200/50 which can be wrong, causing the centering to be off. Also getZoom() may return stale value if initial fitView animation hasn't completed.

title: Use precise type annotation for _count_by_action entries param
description: text.py:140 — entries param typed as dict[str, dict] but values are plan entry dicts with an 'action' key. Bare dict is imprecise compared to other annotations in the file. Minor.
close_reason: Superseded by broader type alias effort across the codebase

title: feat: canvas background click closes detail panel
description: Clicking empty canvas space does nothing when panel is open. Standard dismissal pattern. Fix: add onPaneClick={handleClosePanel} to ReactFlow component in flow-canvas.tsx.

title: feat: support FORCE_COLOR environment variable in _supports_color()
description: R3-MED-13. _supports_color() in text.py only checks NO_COLOR and isatty(). It doesn't support FORCE_COLOR (used by CI tools and testing frameworks to enable color when stdout is not a TTY). Fix: add FORCE_COLOR check between NO_COLOR and isatty: if os.environ.get('NO_COLOR'): return False; if os.environ.get('FORCE_COLOR'): return True; return sys.stdout.isatty(). Standard convention used by chalk, pytest, GitHub Actions, etc. Flagged by: UX (MEDIUM).

title: Validate placeholder count in _inject_plan
description: browser.py:58 — str.replace replaces ALL occurrences of the placeholder. If __DAGSHUND_PLAN_JSON__ appears more than once in the template (e.g. a comment), JSON gets duplicated silently. Validate exactly one occurrence or use replace(..., 1).

title: fix: unhandled promise rejection in usePlanGraph/useResourceGraph
description: Both hooks call toReactFlowElements().then() with no .catch(). If ELK layout fails, the promise rejection is swallowed and the UI shows loading forever. Fix: add .catch() that sets an error state, surface it in the UI.

title: fix: mitigate DNS rebinding on dev server
description: Dev server on 127.0.0.1:3000 has no Host header validation or CORS. DNS rebinding could let a malicious webpage read /api/plan data. Fix: validate Host header (reject anything not 127.0.0.1:3000 or localhost:3000), set Access-Control-Allow-Origin restrictively.

title: Resource graph: support all DABs resource types consistently
description:
## Problem

The resource graph hardcodes UC resource types in multiple places:
- `UC_TYPES` set (catalogs, schemas, volumes, registered_models) — anything not listed falls to workspace
- Hierarchy edge logic checks `resourceType === "catalogs"` and `resourceType === "schemas"` literally
- Phantom catalog detection filters on `extractResourceType(key) === "catalogs"` only
- Jobs are excluded entirely from the resource graph (`isJobEntry` filter)

This means new UC types like `database_catalogs` silently render in the wrong bucket, and jobs don't appear at all.

## Full resource type mapping (from Databricks docs)

Ref: https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/dev-tools/bundles/resources#supported-resources

### Unity Catalog hierarchy (catalog → schema → leaf)
- `catalogs` — top-level UC container
- `database_catalogs` — UC catalog backed by a Lakebase database_instance (same tier as catalogs)
- `schemas` — nest under catalogs
- `volumes`, `registered_models`, `synced_database_tables` — nest under schemas

### Workspace (flat)
- `jobs` — currently excluded from resource graph, should show as single nodes
- `pipelines`, `clusters`, `apps`, `dashboards`, `alerts`, `experiments`
- `model_serving_endpoints`, `sql_warehouses`, `secret_scopes`
- `database_instances` — Lakebase compute/storage, workspace-level
- `postgres_projects`, `postgres_endpoints`, `postgres_branches` — workspace-level
- `quality_monitors` — references UC tables but lives at workspace level

### Cross-graph edges
- `database_catalogs` references `database_instance_name` (workspace) — potential cross-graph edge

## What needs to change

1. **UC_TYPES**: add `database_catalogs` and `synced_database_tables`
2. **Catalog-tier detection**: treat both `catalogs` and `database_catalogs` as catalog-tier nodes (group nodes, phantom detection, hierarchy edges)
3. **Schema-tier detection**: already works for `schemas`, no change needed
4. **Leaf-tier detection**: `synced_database_tables` should behave like volumes (nest under schema)
5. **Jobs in resource graph**: remove the `isJobEntry` filter, render jobs as single nodes in the workspace bucket
6. **Make it consistent**: the categorization and hierarchy logic should be driven by data (sets/maps), not scattered string literals

## Scope
All changes in `src/graph/build-resource-graph.ts` and tests. The rendering components already handle external/phantom nodes generically.

title: Improve node alignment in fan-out/fan-in DAG patterns
description: Nodes in fan-out/fan-in patterns (e.g. data_quality_pipeline) are not perfectly centered on their layer grid. Single nodes like aggregate_results and generate_report can appear off-center relative to their incoming edges. Current ELK config uses BRANDES_KOEPF BALANCED with model-order options which is decent but not perfect. Investigate further ELK tuning or post-layout adjustment to improve vertical centering of nodes relative to their connections.
close_reason: Current ELK layout is sufficient; no clear improvement path

title: visual: thicken border on hovered node for clearer hover feedback
description: When hovering a node, the border should become slightly thicker (e.g. border-3 or outline) to give a clear visual signal of what is being hovered. Applies to job containers, task nodes, resource nodes, and resource group nodes. Currently hover only shows shadow-md which can be subtle.
notes: Implemented hover ring (1.5px box-shadow in diff-state color, debounced 50ms via React state), selection ring (2.5px when detail panel open), and selection dimming (non-connected nodes at 50% opacity, edges at 30%). Hover overrides selection dimming. Added hoverGlow to DiffStateStyles, selectedNodeId/selectedConnectedIds to HoverContext, isHovered/isSelected/dimOpacity to useNodeDimming. Commit: 5a88743

title: fix: make structural diff wrapping responsive to panel width instead of hardcoded 40 chars
description: R3-MED-22. MAX_LINE_CHARS = 40 in components/structural-diff-view.tsx:19 is very aggressive. Comment says 'Conservative for the 380px detail panel' but the panel defaults to 390px and can be resized up to 600px. Content wraps at 40 chars even when the panel is wide. Fix: either increase to 60 chars (fits in 300px min-width at ~7.2px/char mono), or make it responsive by reading the container width via a ref and computing chars that fit. Flagged by: UX (MEDIUM).

title: refactor: use collections.Counter in _count_by_action
description: R3-MED-23. _count_by_action in text.py:132-140 manually builds a count dict with get/set. More Pythonic and immutable: use collections.Counter. Fix: return dict(Counter(entry.get('action') or 'unchanged' for entry in entries.values())). 3-line fix. Flagged by: Code Quality (HIGH).

title: Nodes collapse to (0,0) when hovering after zoom-out
description:
When zoomed out fully and hovering over a node, all nodes collapse to position (0,0) in the top-left corner of the viewport. This was also seen earlier when the requestAnimationFrame ResizeObserver patch was active, but it's now happening without it.

Likely cause: the hover styling logic in DagView applies opacity changes via styledNodes/styledEdges useMemo. Something in that code path or in React Flow's internal re-measure cycle may be resetting node positions when the viewport scale is small enough.

title: Show task-level changes summary on job group nodes
description:
When clicking a job group node (e.g. resources.jobs.data_quality_pipeline) that shows as 'modified', the detail panel currently only shows job-level changes (like depends_on updates) and unchanged job config fields. It does NOT show what changed inside the job at the task level.

For a modified job, the user wants to see a summary of task-level changes directly on the job node's detail panel:
- Added tasks: e.g. "+ check_duplicates, + check_references, + anomaly_detection" (green)
- Removed tasks: e.g. "- old_validation_task" (red)
- Modified tasks: e.g. "~ compute_statistics (depends_on changed)" (amber)

This gives a birds-eye view of what happened inside the job without clicking individual task nodes.

Similarly for added jobs: show all the tasks that were created as part of the job.
For removed jobs: show all the tasks that were deleted.

Implementation considerations:
- Job group nodes already have access to all their child task nodes in the graph
- Need to collect task-level diff states from child nodes and summarize them
- Could be a new section in the detail panel between job-level changes and unchanged config
- The task names should probably be clickable to navigate to that task node

Key files:
- src/components/detail-panel.tsx (add task summary section)
- src/graph/build-plan-graph.ts (job nodes may need references to child task diff states)
- src/types/graph-types.ts (DagNodeData may need child task summary info)

title: Replace vendored dagre.js with proper dependency
description:
The vendored src/vendor/dagre.js (2200 lines of CJS-to-ESM shim) is a hack to work around @dagrejs/dagre v2's ESM dist using dynamic require() that Bun's browser bundler can't trace. Investigate and test alternatives in order of least invasive:

1. **Direct import test** — try importing @dagrejs/dagre directly (no vendor) to see if Bun 1.3.8 handles it now
2. **esbuild prebundle as build step** — `bunx esbuild node_modules/@dagrejs/dagre/dist/dagre.cjs.js --bundle --format=esm --platform=browser --outfile=src/vendor/dagre.js` as a reproducible build:vendor script instead of a committed 2200-line file
3. **esm.sh CDN import** — `import dagre from 'https://esm.sh/@dagrejs/dagre'` to bypass bundler entirely (downside: requires internet)
4. **Switch to elkjs** — proper ESM, recommended by React Flow, more capable. Async API (returns Promise). ~30 line migration.

Test each option against `bun build` and `bun run dev`. Pick the least invasive one that works. If none of 1-3 work cleanly, go with elkjs.
close_reason: Superseded: switching to elkjs instead of fixing dagre vendor shim

title: fix: add loading indicator to FlowCanvas during ELK layout computation
description: R3-HIGH-6. After the plan loads, FlowCanvas shows an empty canvas with zero-count filter badges while ELK computes layout. GraphLayoutState starts as {status:'loading'} but FlowCanvas only handles the 'error' case — when loading, nodes/edges default to EMPTY_NODES/EMPTY_EDGES so React Flow renders blank. Fix: when layoutState.status === 'loading', render a centered 'Computing layout...' message with a pulse animation instead of the empty ReactFlow canvas. The existing LoadingIndicator in App.tsx only covers the stdin fetch phase, not the layout phase. Flagged by: UX (HIGH).

title: fix: remove .yaml/.yml from ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS in cli.ts or add clear error
description: R3-LOW-26. ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS in cli.ts:33 includes .yaml and .yml but parsePlanFromString only handles JSON via JSON.parse. A YAML file would fail with 'Invalid JSON' rather than 'YAML not supported'. Fix: either remove .yaml/.yml from the set, or detect YAML extensions and print a clear error: 'dagshund: YAML input not supported, convert to JSON first'. Flagged by: Code Quality (LOW).

title: Phantom catalog nodes in resource graph
fix: show empty state message when a tab has no nodes
description: R3-MED-15. When a plan has no jobs, the Jobs tab shows an empty React Flow canvas with zero-count filter badges — looks broken. Fix: in FlowCanvas, when baseNodes.length === 0 and layoutState.status === 'ready', render a centered message like 'No jobs in this plan' or 'No resources in this plan' instead of the empty canvas. Need to pass a contextual label (the tab name) to FlowCanvas so the message is specific. Flagged by: UX (MEDIUM).

title: Add a complex multi-job fixture for testing
notes:
Current test fixture is a single job with a handful of tasks. A richer fixture would help stress-test layout, hover dimming, and the detail panel.

## Structure

**Job 1: ETL_PIPELINE (existing job, mostly unchanged)**
- Keep current tasks as-is
- Add a new task that triggers Job 2 via run_job_task referencing ${resources.jobs.DATA_QUALITY_PIPELINE} (DAB cross-job linking syntax)
- This trigger task should be 'added' diff state (new resource)

**Job 2: DATA_QUALITY_PIPELINE (new complex job)**
- 8-10 tasks with realistic Databricks names (validate_schema, check_nulls, compute_statistics, etc.)
- Deep dependency chain (4+ levels) to test ELK layout spacing
- Fan-in pattern: multiple validation tasks feed into a single aggregation task
- Fan-out pattern: one setup task spawns several parallel checks
- Mix of diff states: some added, some modified, one removed, rest unchanged
- Tasks with large change payloads (long field lists) to exercise detail panel scrolling
- Realistic notebook_task / python_wheel_task / run_job_task types

## Goal
Test cross-job edges, complex layout, and all visual states in a single fixture.

title: feat: keyboard navigation for graph nodes
description: All node components are divs with no role, tabIndex, or keyboard handlers. Users cannot open the detail panel without a mouse. Fix: add role='button' tabIndex={0} to node root divs. Wire up onKeyDown (Enter/Space) to open detail panel. Leverage React Flow's nodesFocusable prop.

title: fix: use pytest.skip() instead of silent return in test_browser_mode_file_output
description: R3-MED-18. test_browser_mode_file_output in tests/test_cli.py does 'if not template.exists(): return' which silently passes the test if template.html hasn't been built. CI could show green with zero browser-mode coverage. Fix: replace with pytest.skip('template.html not built; run just template first'). This makes test runners clearly report the skip. Located at tests/test_cli.py around line 72. Flagged by: Code Quality (MEDIUM).

title: fix: detail panel scroll position resets on node switch
description: When clicking Node A then Node B, the detail panel doesn't scroll to top. User sees middle of Node B's content. Fix: add key={selectedNode.resourceKey} to DetailPanel to remount on change, or useEffect to scroll to top when data changes.

title: Detail panel: show full resource state, not just changes
description:
Currently the detail panel only shows diff changes — clicking an unchanged node like 'load' shows nothing useful. The panel should display:

1. **Current state** — all fields/config of the resource as they exist (baseline)
2. **Updates** — fields that changed, showing before → after
3. **Additions** — new fields that were added

This gives full visibility into what a resource looks like, not just what changed. For unchanged nodes the panel should still show the full resource configuration. For modified nodes it should highlight which fields changed within the full context.

title: Fix diff filter toolbar clipping into job nodes on scroll
description: The Added/Modified/Removed filter buttons are rendered inside a React Flow Panel, which is positioned inside the canvas. When scrolling/panning so that a job node overlaps the toolbar area, the buttons clip into the job node. Fix: either raise the z-index of the toolbar panel above nodes, or move it outside the canvas entirely.

title: perf: lazy-mount inactive tab to avoid double ELK layout on initial load
description: R3-MED-7. App.tsx renders both DagView and ResourcesView simultaneously with display:none on the inactive tab. Both hooks fire ELK layout immediately — doubling initial layout time and memory. Fix: lazy-mount the inactive tab using a hasVisited ref per tab. Only render the component when the tab is first selected, then keep it mounted to preserve viewport state. This halves initial load cost for users who never visit one tab. Flagged by: Architect (MEDIUM), Security/Perf (MEDIUM).
close_reason: Won't fix: both tabs are rendered simultaneously by design to preserve viewport state and avoid layout jank on tab switch. This was a deliberate architecture decision.

title: Raise DagshundError on empty plan instead of printing to stderr
description: text.py:216-219 — render_text prints 'plan is empty' to stderr but returns normally (exit 0). In a pipeline context, downstream tools see success when nothing was output. Should raise DagshundError so it exits 1 via the handler in main().

title: task: restyle graph canvas — background, controls, minimap, edges
description:
Apply the new theme to the React Flow canvas layer across both DAG and resource views. Part of epic dagshund-4y6.

## Deliverables
- Canvas background color/pattern for dark and light modes
- React Flow controls panel styled to match theme
- Minimap styled for both modes
- Edge colors and styles updated (stroke width, color, animation for hover)
- Grid dots or subtle pattern if appropriate

title: fix: detail panel header and modified block clipping when resized narrow
description: Two clipping issues when dragging the detail panel narrow: (1) The h2 label clips into the YAML format toggle and X close button — removing truncate doesn't help because break-all looks bad. Need a proper solution (e.g. moving controls to a second row, or using text-wrap with overflow-hidden). (2) In modified diff blocks like notebook_task.base_parameters['sample_size'], the field path never wraps — dragging narrow clips 'update' off-screen and the long path stays on one line. The structural diff view needs word-breaking or responsive wrapping for field paths too.

title: Introduce type aliases for plan data shapes across Python codebase
description:
Replace bare dict annotations with semantic type aliases wherever plan data flows through the Python code.

Candidate aliases (using Python 3.10+ type statement):
- PlanEntry: a single resource entry (has 'action', optional 'changes', 'new_state', etc.)
- Plan: dict of resource key -> PlanEntry
- PlanOutput: top-level object ('plan', 'cli_version', 'plan_version', etc.)

Scope: audit all bare dict annotations in text.py, browser.py, cli.py, and __init__.py. Apply aliases where they add clarity — not every dict needs one, only the plan data shapes.

Supersedes dagshund-8gs which targeted only _count_by_action.

title: feat: add usage examples to --help epilog
description: R3-MED-12. The argparse help text is minimal — no examples. The most common usage pattern (databricks bundle plan -o json | dagshund) is not shown. Fix: add epilog with RawDescriptionHelpFormatter showing: 'dagshund plan.json' (text mode), 'dagshund plan.json -o output.html' (export), 'databricks bundle plan -o json | dagshund' (pipe). Located in cli.py build_parser(). Flagged by: UX (MEDIUM).

title: fix: wrap file read in cli.py with user-friendly error handling
description: R3-HIGH-3. read_plan() in cli.py opens the file with bare open(plan_file) and no exception handling. FileNotFoundError and PermissionError produce raw Python tracebacks — hostile to non-Python users. Fix: wrap in try/except, print clean messages like 'dagshund: file not found: plan.json' to stderr. This can be part of the DagshundError refactor (R3-HIGH-2) — raise DagshundError from the except blocks. Flagged by: UX (HIGH).

title: Migrate from dagre to elkjs for graph layout
description: Replace @dagrejs/dagre (unmaintained since 2015, broken ESM packaging) with elkjs (actively developed, proper ESM, recommended by React Flow). This removes the 2200-line vendor shim entirely. Migration: elkjs has async API so usePlanGraph hook changes from useMemo to useState+useEffect. Files: src/graph/layout-graph.ts, src/hooks/use-plan-graph.ts, src/vendor/dagre.js (delete), src/types/dagre-cjs.d.ts (delete), package.json.
close_reason: elkjs migration complete, -2169 lines, production build verified in browser

title: fix: cross-job run_job_task edges always marked unchanged
description: buildRunJobEdges in build-plan-graph.ts hardcodes diffState: 'unchanged' for cross-job edges. When the source task (e.g. trigger_quality_check) is newly added, the edge should be 'added'. Fix: derive edge diff state from the source task's resolveTaskDiffState.

title: Use sentinel for None vs absent in _render_resource
description: text.py:126-133 — Uses 'is not None' to check old/new values, but Databricks plans can have explicit null. {'old': null, 'new': 'something'} loses the 'changed from null' info. Use a _MISSING sentinel to distinguish absent keys from null values.

title: Interpolate external schema nodes with dashed styling
description: When a volume or registered_model references a schema_name that has no corresponding resources.schemas.* entry in the plan, synthesize a 'phantom' schema node in the resource graph. These nodes represent UC schemas that exist but aren't managed by DABs. Visual treatment: dashed border, muted color, 'external' badge. Implementation: after building schemaNameToKey, second pass over volumes/models where schemaKey is undefined — create interpolated nodes with synthetic IDs like external::catalog.schema, link them catalog → phantom schema → resource. Add a boolean 'external' flag to GraphNode or use a distinct nodeKind.

title: chore: commit bun lockfile

title: Build graph model: Plan to React Flow nodes and edges
description:
Transform a parsed Plan (from parser/) into React Flow-compatible nodes and edges. All pure functions, no React code.

WHAT EXISTS:
- Plan type with record of PlanEntry (keyed by resource path string like 'resources.jobs.etl_pipeline')
- Each PlanEntry has: id, depends_on, action, changes, new_state, remote_state
- DiffState type: added | removed | modified | unchanged
- mapActionToDiffState function in parser/map-diff-state.ts
- Two real fixtures: sample-plan.json (all creates) and mixed-plan.json (updates/skips/removals)

KEY INSIGHT — THE DATA HAS THREE LEVELS:
1. Resource level: top-level plan entries keyed like 'resources.jobs.etl_pipeline'. Each has an action (create/update/delete/skip).
2. Task level: inside new_state.value.tasks, each task has task_key, depends_on (array of {task_key}), notebook_task, etc. These form the actual DAG the user wants to see.
3. Change level: the 'changes' record has keys like 'tasks[task_key=extract].notebook_task.notebook_path' with per-field actions (update/skip/delete). This tells us WHICH tasks and fields changed.

THE GRAPH MODEL MUST:
- Extract tasks from new_state.value.tasks as individual graph nodes
- Build edges from each task's depends_on array (task_key references)
- Determine each task's DiffState by checking the changes record:
  - If changes has 'tasks[task_key=X]' with action=update and only a 'new' field (no 'old') → added
  - If changes has 'tasks[task_key=X]' with action=update and only an 'old' field (no 'new') → removed
  - If changes has 'tasks[task_key=X].some.field' with action=update → modified
  - If all changes for task X are action=skip → unchanged
  - If the resource-level action is 'create' → all tasks are added
- Also produce a job-level metadata node showing the resource itself (name, triggers, etc.) — this is context, not part of the task DAG edges
- Handle multiple jobs in the plan (each is a separate sub-graph)

WHAT TO BUILD in src/graph/:
1. Graph types in src/types/graph-types.ts:
   - GraphNode: id, label, nodeKind ('job' | 'task'), diffState, resourceKey, taskKey?, raw data ref
   - GraphEdge: id, source, target, label?
   - PlanGraph: { readonly nodes: readonly GraphNode[], readonly edges: readonly GraphEdge[] }
2. extractTaskNodes(resourceKey: string, entry: PlanEntry) → readonly GraphNode[]
   - Dig into new_state.value.tasks, create a node per task
   - Compute diffState per task from the changes record
3. extractTaskEdges(resourceKey: string, entry: PlanEntry) → readonly GraphEdge[]
   - For each task with depends_on, create source→target edges using task_key
4. extractJobNode(resourceKey: string, entry: PlanEntry) → GraphNode
   - A single node representing the job itself with its resource-level diffState
5. buildPlanGraph(plan: Plan) → PlanGraph
   - Iterate all plan entries, call the above for each, combine into one graph
6. toReactFlowElements(graph: PlanGraph) → { nodes: Node[], edges: Edge[] }
   - Convert to React Flow format with dagre-computed positions
   - Must handle hundreds of nodes

FIXTURES TO TEST AGAINST:
- tests/fixtures/sample-plan.json — single job, action=create, 4 tasks (extract→transform→load→validate)
- tests/fixtures/mixed-plan.json — single job, action=update, changes include: transform notebook_path updated, aggregate task added (new only), validate task removed (old only), extract/load unchanged (all skips)

CONSTRAINTS:
- All readonly types, all pure functions, no classes
- Functions under 20 lines, verb-first camelCase naming
- Use Result type for anything that can fail
- No any, no type assertions without comment
- Dagre is a new dependency — @dagrejs/dagre
- new_state.value is typed as unknown in the schema — will need careful narrowing (Zod parse or type guard) to extract tasks safely

title: Fix task extraction failing on numeric job_id in real plan data
Visualize added/removed edges on modified tasks
description:
Currently edge diff state is inferred from endpoint nodes only (resolveEdgeDiffState in diff-state-styles.ts). If either endpoint is added/removed, the edge gets that state. But when a MODIFIED task changes its depends_on array (adds or removes a dependency), the edge has no diff state because both endpoint nodes still exist.

Example: task A depends_on [B, C]. After modification, task A depends_on [B, D]. The edge A→C should show as red/removed (ghost edge), and A→D should show as green/added. Currently A→C just disappears and A→D appears as gray/unchanged.

Implementation requires:
1. Compare old depends_on (from change old_value) vs new depends_on (from new_value) for modified tasks
2. Create ghost edges for removed dependencies — these edges exist in old state but not new state. Render red with reduced opacity.
3. Mark newly added edges (exist in new state but not old state) as added. Render green.
4. GraphEdge type needs a diffState field: 'added' | 'removed' | 'unchanged'
5. toFlowEdges() in layout-graph.ts needs to use the edge's own diffState when present, falling back to the current endpoint-inference logic

Key files:
- src/types/graph-types.ts (add diffState to GraphEdge)
- src/graph/extract-tasks.ts (extract old/new depends_on from changes)
- src/graph/build-plan-graph.ts (compare old vs new depends_on, create ghost edges, mark new edges)
- src/graph/layout-graph.ts (toFlowEdges should respect edge-level diffState)
- src/components/diff-state-styles.ts (resolveEdgeDiffState already handles the styling, just needs to check edge's own diffState first)

title: Replace Any type aliases with proper typed structures
description:
## Problem

The foundational type aliases in `src/dagshund/__init__.py` use `Any`, violating the project's "unknown over any" guideline:

```python
from typing import Any

type ResourceChange = dict[str, Any]
type ResourceChangeMap = dict[str, ResourceChange]
type Plan = dict[str, Any]
```

This means every downstream access (`data.get("plan", {})`, `entry.get("action", "")`, etc.) is completely untyped. The TypeScript side has Zod schema validation at boundaries; the Python side has nothing equivalent.

## What to Do

Replace `Any`-based type aliases with properly typed structures. Options:

### Option A: TypedDict (recommended)
```python
from typing import TypedDict, NotRequired

class FieldChange(TypedDict):
    action: NotRequired[str]
    old: NotRequired[object]
    new: NotRequired[object]

class ResourceChange(TypedDict):
    action: NotRequired[str]
    changes: NotRequired[dict[str, FieldChange]]

type ResourceChangeMap = dict[str, ResourceChange]

class Plan(TypedDict):
    plan: NotRequired[ResourceChangeMap]
    cli_version: NotRequired[str]
    # ... other known top-level keys
```

Note: TypedDict uses `class` syntax but is NOT OOP — it's purely a type-level construct with no runtime behavior, no methods, no inheritance hierarchy. It's the Python equivalent of a TypeScript `interface` or a Zod schema. This is consistent with the project philosophy (same as how the TS side uses `interface` and `type` without it being OOP).

### Option B: Use `object` instead of `Any`
Minimal change: replace `Any` with `object` everywhere, forcing runtime type narrowing (`isinstance` checks) before use. Less ergonomic but maximally strict.

## Files That Will Need Updates
- `src/dagshund/__init__.py` — type definitions + `parse_plan` return type
- `src/dagshund/text.py` — all functions consuming ResourceChange/Plan
- `src/dagshund/browser.py` — `_inject_plan` parameter type
- `src/dagshund/cli.py` — if Plan type changes

## Acceptance Criteria
- No `Any` import or usage in `src/dagshund/`
- All type annotations use the new typed structures
- `just check` passes (including `ty` type checker)
- Existing tests pass without modification
close_reason: Won't fix: the plan data is genuinely Any from our perspective — we don't control the Databricks CLI schema. Value fields (old/new/remote) are polymorphic (strings, ints, dicts, lists). TypedDict would still need Any for those, giving minimal benefit for the complexity. Runtime isinstance guards from the input hardening issue handle malformed data.

title: Split create/delete diffs into per-field entries
description:
Redesign detail panel to show three clearly labeled sections for any node: Added (create), Modified (update), Unchanged.

## Added (create) — for new tasks or new fields on modified tasks
Instead of one giant JSON blob, break into individual field entries. Each field (depends_on, notebook_task, task_key) gets its own card with green + styling. For a fully new task, the header says "this task was created" and each field is shown as its own create entry.

## Modified (update) — for changed fields
Current behavior is good: shows old value with - red, new value with + green (structural diff). Additionally:
- If a key is REMOVED from a modified task (e.g. depends_on deleted entirely), show it in red with - as its own entry in this section
- If a key is ADDED to a modified task, show it in green with + as its own entry

## Unchanged — for fields that didn't change
Renamed from "Unmodified Configuration". Shows resource state fields not involved in any change, neutral styling.

## Removed (delete) — for deleted tasks or removed fields
Mirror of create: each field gets its own card in red with - styling. "This task was deleted, here's what it had."

## Section ordering
Added → Modified → Unchanged (or just the relevant ones depending on node diffState)

## Key files
- src/components/detail-panel.tsx (main rendering logic, ChangeEntry, ResourceStateView)
- src/utils/structural-diff.ts (computeStructuralDiff)
- src/components/structural-diff-view.tsx (StructuralDiffView)

Supersedes dagshund-jze.

title: Job container node + left-to-right DAG layout
description:
Transform the current flat node layout into a Databricks-style DAG:

1. **Job as group/container node**: Use React Flow's group node (parentId) so the job is a labeled container with task nodes rendered inside it. The job node should show the job name as a header and have a border styled by its diff state.

2. **Left-to-right ELK layout**: Change `elk.direction` from `DOWN` to `RIGHT`. Tune spacing so the primary dependency chain flows horizontally and branching/parallel tasks fan vertically.

3. **Smooth curved edges**: Use React Flow's `smoothstep` or `bezier` edge type instead of default straight edges.

4. **Linear preference**: Configure ELK to prefer linear/straight paths for the longest chain, pushing branches to the side.

### Files to modify
- `src/graph/layout-graph.ts` — flip direction, add group node support, adjust spacing
- `src/graph/build-plan-graph.ts` — set parentId on task nodes pointing to job node
- `src/components/job-node.tsx` — restyle as container (larger, acts as boundary)
- `src/components/task-node.tsx` — update handle positions (Left/Right instead of Top/Bottom)
- `src/App.tsx` — update edge options for smooth curves
- Tests need updating for new layout direction and group node structure

### Reference
Databricks job overview DAG: left-to-right flow, linear spine, vertical branching.

title: V1 Vision: Databricks-style plan visualizer
description:
## Vision

Dagshund V1 is a local tool that accepts piped `databricks bundle plan -o json` output, opens a browser window, and shows the plan as an interactive DAG with diff highlighting.

Eventually this becomes a VS Code plugin, but V1 is browser-based.

### Visual Model (inspired by Databricks job DAG UI)

- **Job as container**: Each job is a labeled container/group node. Tasks live inside it.
- **Tasks as DAG**: Tasks are rounded-rectangle cards connected by smooth curved edges showing dependency relationships.
- **Left-to-right layout**: The primary dependency chain flows horizontally left→right. Branching/parallel tasks fan vertically (up/down) off the main spine. Prefer linear edge paths.
- **Reference**: Databricks job overview UI — see `/tmp/databricks-dag.png` or https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/assets/images/example-job-overview-3afc40004602e65c871bd67edfa87f9b.png

### Diff Color Coding

- **Green** (border + subtle bg): New component (added)
- **Yellow/Amber** (border + subtle bg): Changed component (modified)
- **Red + reduced opacity (40%)**: Deleted component (removed)
- **Neutral**: Unchanged component

### Interaction Model

- **Click task** → Right-side slide-in detail panel showing all changes for that task
- **Hover task** → Visual highlighting of the task and its immediate dependency edges
- Pan, zoom, minimap for navigation

### Data Flow

```
databricks bundle plan -o json | dagshund
  → Bun server reads stdin, serves frontend
  → Frontend fetches /api/plan
  → Zod parse + validate
  → Transform to graph model
  → ELK layout (left-to-right, layered)
  → React Flow render with custom nodes
```

### What's Done

- Stdin pipe → server → browser auto-open
- Zod parsing and validation
- Graph model (Plan → nodes + edges)
- Diff state resolution and color coding
- ELK layout (currently top-to-bottom, needs flip)
- React Flow rendering with custom job/task nodes
- Tests (51 passing, 127 assertions)

### What's Remaining for V1

1. Job as container node (React Flow group/parent)
2. Left-to-right layout with linear preference
3. Click task → detail panel
4. Hover task → highlighting

title: refactor: eliminate type drift and naming inconsistencies
description: Multiple type/naming inconsistencies: (1) DagNodeData (graph-types.ts:42-52) manually copies GraphNode minus id — should be Omit<GraphNode,'id'>. (2) Same 9-field data spreading written 3x in layout-graph.ts — extract toNodeData helper. (3) LayoutResult type defined identically in use-plan-graph.ts and use-resource-graph.ts — share one definition. (4) formatJobLabel vs extractResourceName — identical last-dot-segment logic with different names. (5) extractTypeBadge vs extractResourceType — same key extraction, different names. (6) getEdgeStyle uses inline literal type instead of EdgeDiffState. (7) EdgeDiffState not exported from types barrel. (8) 'changed' vs 'modified' for the same concept across structural-diff vs graph types.

title: fix: harden script tag escaping with universal < replacement
description: R3-MED-24. _escape_for_script_tag in browser.py and escapeForScriptTag in cli.ts/build-template.ts only escape </script and <\!-- patterns. The industry-standard approach is simpler and comprehensive: replace ALL < with the unicode escape. Python: content.replace('<', '\\u003c'). JS: content.replace(/</g, '\\u003c'). Current escaping IS functional (prevents </script tag closure), but the universal approach is defense-in-depth with fewer edge cases. This fix should be made in the shared html-assembler.ts module (see R3-HIGH-1) and in browser.py. Flagged by: Security/Perf (LOW x3), but promoted to MEDIUM because the fix is trivial and eliminates an entire class of injection vectors.

title: Render ghost nodes and ghost edges for deleted tasks and changed dependencies
description:
Two related gaps in DAG visualization, implemented together:

**Ghost nodes (deleted tasks):** Tasks that are deleted (exist in changes record with action=delete but not in new_state) currently don't appear as nodes in the DAG. They should be rendered as red nodes with reduced opacity (removed diff state styling). Requires extracting deleted task keys from the changes record in buildEntryGraph and creating ghost nodes for them.

**Ghost edges (changed dependencies):** When a modified task changes its depends_on array, removed edges just disappear and new edges appear gray/unchanged. Example: task A depends_on [B, C] → [B, D]. Edge A→C should render red/removed, edge A→D should render green/added. Requires comparing old vs new depends_on from changes record, creating ghost edges for removed deps, and marking new edges as added. GraphEdge needs a diffState field.

title: Design and implement CI/CD pipeline
description:
Design and implement GitLab CI/CD pipeline for dagshund.

## Current State
- .gitlab-ci.yml exists with SAST + secret detection on every push
- No linting, typechecking, testing, or build verification in CI
- No release/publish pipeline
- 400 CI minutes/month (GitLab free tier)

## Open Questions

### Pipeline structure
Two trigger points discussed:
1. Every push to main — security only (already have)
2. On tags (releases) — full pipeline

But adding HTML export testing (Playwright) changes the picture — Playwright needs a browser runtime, which is heavier. Do we:
- Run Playwright only on tags?
- Run a lighter HTML validation (file exists, correct structure) on every push, Playwright only on tags?
- Skip the per-push security and only run everything on tags to save minutes?

### HTML export testing
Want to verify the HTML export actually works, not just that it builds. Options discussed:
1. Basic: generate HTML from fixture, check it's non-empty and contains expected markers
2. Middle ground: parse HTML in Bun, verify script tags and embedded data
3. Playwright: headless browser, check no JS errors, DOM elements render, click interactions work

Playwright is the real acceptance test but adds complexity:
- Needs playwright install (downloads browsers)
- Heavier Docker image or extra CI step
- More CI minutes per run
- But only runs on tags, so maybe acceptable

### Release cycle
- PyPI publish step needed (placeholder for now)
- Manual trigger vs automatic on tag push
- Need to set up PyPI trusted publisher or API token

### Minute budget
- 400 min/month on GitLab free tier
- Security scan per push is cheap
- Full pipeline (lint + typecheck + test + build + Playwright) per release — how expensive?
- User is sole contributor, runs just check locally before every commit

title: Job sorting for large bundle files
description: Large bundle plans with many jobs render in arbitrary order, making it hard to find specific jobs. Add sorting options (alphabetical, by diff state, by task count) to the DAG view so users can orient themselves quickly in big graphs.

title: Clean up action constants in text.py
description: The action-related constants at module level in src/dagshund/text.py are scattered and their relationship is implicit. UPDATE_ACTIONS is public (no underscore) while the related _DISPLAY_ACTIONS, _ACTION_STYLES, and _DEFAULT_STYLE are private. The dict.fromkeys spread in _ACTION_STYLES is clever but not immediately readable. Consider: (1) making UPDATE_ACTIONS private (_UPDATE_ACTIONS) since it's only used within the module, (2) grouping all action constants together with a clear comment block, (3) possibly replacing the dict.fromkeys spread with something more explicit.
notes: UPDATE_ACTIONS is imported in tests/test_text.py (line 13) for parametrized test generation. No other production code uses it. Making it private (_UPDATE_ACTIONS) would require updating the test import but is otherwise safe.

title: Decompose oversized functions and replace mutation with functional patterns
description:
## Problem

Four functions exceed the 20-line guideline, and three functions use imperative mutation (list.append, dict.setdefault) instead of functional patterns (generators, comprehensions). These overlap — the oversized functions are often the ones with mutation.

## Oversized Functions

### 1. `_render_resource` — ~34 logic lines
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around lines 93-132
- Handles both the resource header AND field-level change details
- **Fix**: Extract field-change rendering into a separate function (e.g., `_render_field_changes`)
- Also uses `list.append()` mutation — replace with `yield` (make it a generator) or build via comprehension

### 2. `build_parser` — ~29 lines
- **File**: `src/dagshund/cli.py`, around lines 18-47
- Largely declarative argparse configuration, so complexity is low
- **Fix**: Could extract argument groups into helper functions, but this is low priority given it's purely declarative

### 3. `main` — ~26 lines
- **File**: `src/dagshund/cli.py`, around lines 72-97
- Handles argument parsing, branching (browser vs text), error handling, exit
- **Fix**: Extract browser branch (lines ~82-90) and text branch (lines ~91-94) into separate functions

### 4. `render_text` — borderline at ~21 lines
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around lines 197-219
- Right at the limit, may not need changes after other refactoring

## Mutation Patterns to Replace

### 1. `_render_resource` — `lines.append()`
- Builds a `list[str]` via repeated `.append()` calls across ~30 lines
- **Fix**: Convert to a generator that `yield`s lines, or build via comprehension/concatenation

### 2. `_group_by_resource_type` — `dict.setdefault().append()`
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around lines 156-162
- Builds a dict of lists imperatively
- **Fix**: Use `itertools.groupby` on sorted input, or a comprehension-based approach

### 3. `_print_summary` — `summary_parts.append()`
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around lines 182-189
- Builds a list then joins it
- **Fix**: Replace with a generator expression inside `", ".join(...)`

## Acceptance Criteria
- No function in `src/dagshund/` exceeds 20 lines of logic
- No `list.append()` or `dict.setdefault()` mutation patterns — use generators, comprehensions, or `itertools`
- All existing tests pass without modification
- `just check` passes
close_reason: All mutation patterns replaced, all functions under 20 lines, pattern matching for type dispatch

title: Render DAG with diff-state colored nodes in React Flow
description:
Wire the graph model into React Flow rendering with visual diff-state distinction. Minimal styling — just enough to tell states apart.

DEPENDS ON: dagshund-ebk (graph model must exist first)

DATA CONTEXT:
The graph model produces three kinds of nodes:
- Job nodes (nodeKind='job'): represent the resource itself, contain metadata (name, triggers, compute)
- Task nodes (nodeKind='task'): individual tasks within a job, form the DAG via depends_on edges
- The user wants to see BOTH: the job as context/container and the tasks as the interactive DAG

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. Custom React Flow node components:
   - src/components/task-node.tsx — renders a task node with diff-state color coding:
     - added: green border/accent
     - removed: red border, reduced opacity (40%)
     - modified: amber/yellow accent
     - unchanged: neutral/default (zinc/gray)
   - src/components/job-node.tsx — renders job metadata (name, etc.) as a header/container node
   Keep it simple — colored border or background tint is enough for now. No fancy visuals yet.

2. Update App.tsx to:
   - Accept a Plan object as prop or via context
   - Call graph model functions to get React Flow nodes/edges
   - Pass custom node types to ReactFlow
   - For now, use a hardcoded test plan or accept via window.__DAGSHUND_PLAN__ global (stdin feature will wire this up)

3. Custom hook (src/hooks/use-plan-graph.ts):
   - Takes Plan, returns { nodes, edges } ready for React Flow
   - Memoized with useMemo

CONSTRAINTS:
- Function components only, no classes
- All readonly where possible
- Register custom node types properly with React Flow nodeTypes (must be stable reference, not inline object)
- Must handle empty plan gracefully (no nodes = empty canvas, not a crash)
- Dark background (zinc-950 already set in App.tsx) — colors should work on dark
- Handle multiple jobs producing separate sub-graphs within the same canvas
close_reason: Custom job/task nodes with diff-state coloring, usePlanGraph hook, App wired with mixed-plan fixture. 51 tests passing, tsc clean.

title: Support detailed exit codes for CI usage (like terraform plan)
description:
Support detailed exit codes for CI usage, similar to terraform plan -detailed-exitcode.

## Exit codes (opt-in via flag)
- Exit 0: plan parsed, no changes (all resources have action 'skip')
- Exit 1: error (bad input, missing file, etc.)
- Exit 2: plan parsed, changes detected (at least one resource has a non-skip action)

## Key discovery
Databricks includes ALL managed resources in plan output, even unchanged ones. Unchanged resources have `"action": "skip"` at the top level. So `plan["plan"]` being non-empty does NOT mean drift.

Drift detection logic:
```python
has_changes = any(
    entry.get("action") not in ("skip", "")
    for entry in plan.values()
)
```

## Fixture
`fixtures/no-changes-plan.json` — real plan output where nothing changed. All 8 resources have `"action": "skip"`.

## Open questions
- Flag name: --detailed-exitcode (terraform convention) vs shorter alternative
- Applies to both text and browser modes
- Interaction with dagshund-cfx: empty plan (no plan key / empty dict) is still exit 1 (error). A plan full of skips is exit 0 (success, no changes). These are different situations.

title: refactor: replace typing.Any with object in text.py _format_value
description: R3-MED-19. text.py imports typing.Any and uses it in _format_value(value: Any). Python's object type is the equivalent of TypeScript's unknown — requires explicit narrowing before use. Fix: change 'from typing import Any' to remove the import, change parameter to _format_value(value: object) -> str. The isinstance checks inside already narrow correctly. Flagged by: Code Quality (MEDIUM).

title: feat: add high-contrast accessibility theme
Rename non-verb-first functions to match naming convention
description:
## Problem

Three private functions in `src/dagshund/text.py` use noun/adjective names instead of the required verb-first naming convention. The project guidelines require: "Descriptive names. Verb-first (extractJobTaskEdges not getEdges). No abbreviations."

## Functions to Rename

### 1. `_action_style` → `_resolve_action_style`
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around line 54
- Returns `(color, symbol)` tuple for an action string
- "action_style" is a noun phrase; "resolve" makes it verb-first
- Used in: `_render_resource`, `_print_summary`

### 2. `_display_action` → `_map_display_action`
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around line 59
- Maps internal action names to user-facing display names
- "display" is ambiguous (noun or verb); "map" clarifies intent
- Used in: `_render_resource`

### 3. `_all_unchanged` → `_check_all_unchanged`
- **File**: `src/dagshund/text.py`, around line 192
- Returns bool indicating whether every resource is unchanged
- "all_unchanged" is an adjective; "check" makes it verb-first
- Alternative: `_is_all_unchanged` (Python predicate convention)
- Used in: `render_text`

## Scope
- Rename the function definitions
- Update all call sites within `src/dagshund/text.py`
- Update all references in test files (`tests/test_text.py`) — the tests import these private functions directly
- Grep the entire repo to make sure no other references are missed

## Acceptance Criteria
- All three functions renamed with verb-first names
- All call sites and test references updated
- `just check` passes
- No stale references to old names anywhere in the repo

title: Color resource graph edges by diff state
description: On the resource graph page, all edges render as default grey regardless of the connected nodes' diff states. The plan graph correctly colors edges via getEdgeStyle, but the resource graph layout (layoutResourceGraph) does not apply diff-state styling to edges. Fix: ensure resource graph edges use the same diff-state-derived colors as the plan graph.

title: Add debug logging and verbose output
description:
Two distinct layers of additional output:

1. **Debug logging** (stdlib logging module, --debug / -d flag or DAGSHUND_DEBUG=1)
   - Traces the full pipeline: input source (file/stdin), JSON parse result, plan extraction, resource grouping, rendering path (text vs browser)
   - Uses Python logging at DEBUG level — conventional, composable with other tools
   - Outputs to stderr so it doesn't interfere with stdout rendering

2. **Verbose mode** (--verbose / -v flag)
   - Extra user-facing information: resource counts per type, field change counts, timing, plan metadata details
   - Not developer diagnostics — useful operational detail for the user

Both should be independent flags that can be combined. Debug implies verbose.

Scope: full pipeline coverage across cli.py, __init__.py, text.py, browser.py.

title: perf: fix O(n²) algorithms in layout-graph.ts
description: Two algorithmic inefficiencies: (1) topologicalSortTasks (lines 99-117) uses Array.shift() which is O(n) per call, making the overall BFS O(n²) instead of O(n+E). Fix: use index-based queue (maintain front pointer). (2) buildElkCompoundGraph (lines 170-180) filters edges with O(T*E) linear scans via .some(). Fix: build a Set of task IDs per group and use .has() for O(1) lookup. Both are theoretical concerns for typical Databricks DAG sizes but easy fixes.

title: fix: edge colors don't update on theme toggle
description: getEdgeStyle in diff-state-styles.ts calls getComputedStyle to resolve CSS variables at layout time, baking in the values. When the user toggles theme, edges retain old colors while nodes update. Fix: use var(--edge-added) etc as inline style values instead of resolved colors, so the browser resolves them reactively.

title: Consolidate test fixtures into root-level fixtures/ directory

title: Browser UI: handle all-skip plans and display 'unchanged' instead of 'skip'

title: refactor: replace sys.exit() in library functions with DagshundError exceptions
description: R3-HIGH-2. This makes the code untestable without subprocess isolation and impossible to use as a library. Fix: create a DagshundError exception class. Have library functions raise it instead of calling sys.exit. Let main() in cli.py be the only place that catches DagshundError and calls sys.exit with the message. title: Hover task → highlight node and dependency edges
description:
When a user hovers over a task node, visually highlight that task and its immediate dependency edges.

### Behavior
- Mouse enters task node → highlight the node (brighter border or glow) and all edges directly connected to it (both incoming and outgoing)
- Non-connected edges and nodes should dim slightly to create contrast
- Mouse leaves → restore normal styling
- Should feel responsive (no animation delay on enter, subtle fade on leave is OK)

### Implementation approach
- Use React Flow's `onNodeMouseEnter` / `onNodeMouseLeave` callbacks
- Track hovered node ID in state
- Apply conditional CSS classes to edges based on whether they connect to the hovered node
- Could use React Flow's `useEdges` or custom edge styling

### Files to modify
- `src/App.tsx` or `src/hooks/` — hover state management
- `src/components/task-node.tsx` — hover visual feedback on the node itself
- Edge styling in App.tsx or a custom edge component

### Styling
Highlighted edges: brighter color (white or accent). Dimmed elements: reduced opacity. Keep it subtle — this is a wayfinding aid, not a light show.
close_reason: Suppression moved to inline script in index.html; error overlay no longer fires
